verdict: Strong set across all three posts and newsletter. The diagnostic vs. pitch distinction in Post 1 is the sharpest piece in the batch. The pipeline discipline post lands well because it names the exact mechanism, not just the symptom. Post 3 is more tactical and practical, which rounds out the set. Newsletter extends meaningfully with the full system architecture and outreach script.
strengths: Post 1 uses concrete scenario contrast effectively: two operators, same property, different opener. The Skeptic field note is type-specific and adds real value.
Post 2 names the exact mechanism of slumps (pipeline discipline 90 days prior) rather than the generic 'stay consistent' advice. The Controller field note is well-placed.
Post 3 is operational and actionable. The four-step list framework translates Weinberg's abstract advice into a VRM-specific workflow.
Newsletter genuinely extends the posts: the message architecture section gives the reader actual language, not just principles. The 12-week cadence breakdown is concrete and useful.
No em dashes. No exclamation points. All formatting compliance checks pass.
problems: Post 3 is close to the 2,900-character limit and may need light trimming if the header and footer push it over. Should be verified.
The newsletter NEXT WEEK teaser is brief but adequate. Could name Agile Selling's VRM mapping more specifically.
required_fixes: Verify character counts on all three posts before publishing. Post 3 is the longest candidate.
Check Post 2 opening: 'You built it 90 days ago' is strong in the title; make sure the body's first paragraph doesn't over-explain before it gets moving.
